UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile information and application preferences within Careingo.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

username.description: The user's chosen display name or alias.

email.description: The user's email address, primarily for identification purposes. Authentication is handled by an external system.

avatarUrl.description: URL to the user's selected avatar image or a default avatar.

languagePreference.description: The user's preferred language for the application interface (e.g., 'en', 'es').

themePreference.description: The user's preferred visual theme for the application (e.g., 'light', 'dark').

registrationRank.description: A numeric rank indicating the user's order of registration, showcasing early adopters.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was initially created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was last updated.

adPersonalizationEnabled.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the user has consented to personalized advertising.

Track.title: Track
Track.description: Defines the static information for a core care track (e.g., Fitness, Nutrition, Behavior, Study).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Track entity.

name.description: The name of the care track (e.g., 'Fitness', 'Nutrition').

description.description: A brief description of what the track entails.

totalStages.description: The total number of stages within this track (e.g., 30).

Stage.title: Stage
Stage.description: Defines the static information for a specific stage within a track.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Stage entity.

trackId.description: Reference to the Track this stage belongs to. (Relationship: Track 1:N Stage)

stageNumber.description: The sequential number of the stage within its track (e.g., 1 to 30).

title.description: The title or short name of the stage (e.g., 'Day 1', 'Introduction to Mindfulness').

defaultDescription.description: A general, static description of the stage's purpose or content.

stageImageUrl.description: URL to an image representing this stage, if applicable.

UserTrackProgress.title: UserTrackProgress
UserTrackProgress.description: Tracks a user's overall progression through a specific care track.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserTrackProgress entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this progress record. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserTrackProgress)

trackId.description: Reference to the Track this progress record is for. (Relationship: Track 1:N UserTrackProgress)

currentOpenStageNumber.description: The highest stage number that is currently unlocked and accessible for the user in this track.

lastDailyCompletionDate.description: The date when a stage in this specific track was last completed, used to enforce the one-stage-per-track-per-day rule.

completedStageNumbers.description: An array of stage numbers that the user has successfully completed within this track.

UserDailyTask.title: UserDailyTask
UserDailyTask.description: Represents a specific daily task or challenge assigned to a user for a given stage and date. This holds both AI-generated content and user-defined fitness plans.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserDailyTask entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile this task is assigned to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserDailyTask)

trackId.description: Reference to the Track this task belongs to. (Relationship: Track 1:N UserDailyTask)

stageNumber.description: The stage number within the track that this daily task corresponds to.

taskDate.description: The specific date for which this daily task is assigned or completed.

content.description: The actual content of the task. This can be AI-generated text for Nutrition/Behavior/Study tracks, or user-defined fitness activities/rest day details for the Fitness track.

isCompleted.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the user has completed this daily task.

completedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user completed this daily task.

aiGeneratedMetadata.description: Optional JSON string containing metadata or parameters used during the AI content generation for this task.

UserPerformance.title: UserPerformance
UserPerformance.description: Stores aggregated performance metrics for a user, used for progress tracking and leaderboards.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserPerformance entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile associated with these performance metrics. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 UserPerformance)

overallProgressPercentage.description: The user's overall progress across all tracks, expressed as a percentage (0-100).

currentDailyStreak.description: The number of consecutive days the user has completed at least one task.

longestDailyStreak.description: The user's longest achieved streak of consecutive task completions.

lastStreakUpdateDate.description: The last date when the user's streak was checked or updated.

globalRank.description: The user's current global ranking on the leaderboard, based on overall progress or other metrics.

lastUpdatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when these performance metrics were last updated.

Achievement.title: Achievement
Achievement.description: Defines the static information for an achievement badge that users can earn.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Achievement entity.

name.description: The name of the achievement (e.g., 'First Track Complete', '7 Day Streak').

description.description: A detailed description of the achievement and how it is earned.

badgeImageUrl.description: URL to the graphic image representing the achievement badge.

criteria.description: A programmatic or human-readable string defining the conditions required to earn this achievement.

UserAchievement.title: UserAchievement
UserAchievement.description: Records an instance of a user having earned a specific achievement.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserAchievement entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who earned the achievement. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserAchievement)

achievementId.description: Reference to the Achievement definition that was earned. (Relationship: Achievement 1:N UserAchievement)

earnedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the achievement was earned.

UserReminder.title: UserReminder
UserReminder.description: Stores personalized reminder settings configured by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserReminder entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who set this reminder. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserReminder)

message.description: The customizable message for the reminder notification.

scheduledTime.description: The time of day when the reminder should be sent (e.g., '09:00:00').

isEnabled.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this reminder is currently active.

trackId.description: Optional reference to a specific Track if the reminder is track-specific. (Relationship: Track 1:N UserReminder)

daysOfWeek.description: An array of strings indicating the days of the week on which the reminder should be active (e.g., ['monday', 'wednesday']).

ResourceArticle.title: ResourceArticle
ResourceArticle.description: Contains informational content for the Resource Hub, including tips, health information, and legal documents.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ResourceArticle entity.

title.description: The title of the article or resource.

content.description: The full content of the article, which can be in a rich text format like HTML or Markdown.

category.description: The category the article belongs to (e.g., 'Health Tips', 'Legal', 'Support').

url.description: Optional URL for external resources or a canonical link to this article, if hosted externally.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the resource article was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the resource article was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Documents are identified by the user's authentication UID. This is private data, owned by the user.
Defines the static information for core care tracks (e.g., Fitness, Nutrition). This is global, public data.
Defines the static information for a specific stage within a track. The 'trackId' field in the document serves as a denormalized reference. This is global, public data.

params.name: trackId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Track.

params.name: stageId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Stage.

definition.description: Tracks a user's overall progression through a specific care track. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the UserProfile who owns this progress record.

params.name: userTrackProgressId
params.description: The unique identifier for this user's track progress record.

definition.description: Represents a specific daily task or challenge assigned to a user for a given stage and date. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the UserProfile to whom this task is assigned.

params.name: userDailyTaskId
params.description: The unique identifier for this specific daily task.

definition.description: Stores aggregated performance metrics for a user. This is a singleton document per user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the UserProfile associated with these performance metrics.

definition.description: Defines the static information for an achievement badge that users can earn. This is global, public data.
Records an instance of a user having earned a specific achievement. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the UserProfile who earned the achievement.

params.name: userAchievementId
params.description: The unique identifier for this user's earned achievement record.

definition.description: Stores personalized reminder settings configured by a user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the UserProfile who set this reminder.

params.name: userReminderId
params.description: The unique identifier for this user's specific reminder.

definition.description: Contains informational content for the Resource Hub. This is global, public data.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and debuggable, strictly adhering to the core design principles and strategy mandates. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization (Strategy A):**
All user-specific data is structured under `/users/{userId}/...`. For every document in these user-specific subcollections (e.g., `UserTrackProgress`, `UserDailyTask`, `UserAchievement`, `UserReminder`), the `userId` field is explicitly denormalized within the document. This is critical for authorization independence. Security rules can directly compare `request.auth.uid` with `resource.data.userId` without needing to perform `get()` operations on parent documents. This approach allows for atomic writes (e.g., creating a user profile and their first track progress in a single batch) and greatly simplifies rule logic, making it easier to reason about and debug. For instance, a rule for `/users/{userId}/trackProgresses/{userTrackProgressId}` can simply be `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId;` or `request.auth.uid == userTrackProgressId.userId;` (where `userTrackProgressId` is the wildcard and `userId` is the denormalized field in the document, which must match the path wildcard). Static, global entities like `Track`, `Stage`, `Achievement`, and `ResourceArticle` naturally have no authorization dependencies and are globally readable.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters) (Principle 4 & Strategy B):**
This structure ensures Query Authorization Predictability (QAPs) by implementing **Structural Segregation (Strategy B)**. 
1.  **Public/Global Data:** Collections like `/tracks`, `/achievements`, and `/resourceArticles` contain static, universally accessible data. Any `list` query on these collections is inherently secure because all documents within them share the same 'public read' security posture. Rules for these collections are simple: `allow read: if true;`.
2.  **Private/User-Owned Data:** All user-specific data is located under `/users/{userId}/...`. When a client queries `/users/{request.auth.uid}/dailyTasks`, the path itself acts as an implicit filter, ensuring that only tasks belonging to the authenticated user are retrieved. The security rules will then verify that `request.auth.uid` matches the `{userId}` wildcard in the path. Because all documents within these user-specific paths belong to that user, a `list` operation on these subcollections is always secure and doesn't require rules to filter results, which is inefficient and insecure. This segregation prevents scenarios where a single collection might contain mixed access permissions, thus keeping rules concise and performant.

**User Request for Realtime Database:** The user explicitly requested to use Realtime Database instead of Firestore. However, the provided schema specifically requires `firestorePaths` and adheres to Firestore collection modeling. Therefore, this response provides a Firestore structure as per the schema, assuming a Firestore context. If a Realtime Database structure is required, a different schema or a manual translation of these principles would be necessary.
